title: reseda luteola var. gussonei
intro: Reseda luteola var. gussonei, also known as Golden Madder or Wild Madder, is a variety of the Reseda luteola plant, a flowering plant in the borage family native to Europe. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, due to the presence of alkaloids and other compounds. The health benefits of Reseda luteola var. gussonei include its potential to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, and it has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments.
